meta.purpose: One-sentence statement of what this KDNA helps an AI agent judge in this domain.
meta.load_condition: Load when the user asks for <specific task type that triggers this domain>.

axioms.one_sentence: A specific, contestable principle this domain claims about judgment.
axioms.full_statement: Expand into 2-3 sentences. Be specific. Avoid 'X is important' style. Two experts in this field should be able to agree on whether this axiom applies to a given situation.
axioms.why: What would the agent get wrong without this axiom? (Be specific.)
applies_when: describe situation 1 where this axiom guides judgment correctly
does_not_apply_when: describe situation where applying this axiom would mislead the agent
describe another case where the axiom should step back
axioms.failure_risk: What bad judgment results if the agent applies this axiom OUTSIDE its scope? (Be specific.)

ontology.one_sentence: One-sentence description of a domain-specific concept.
ontology.essence: What this concept fundamentally is. Operational, not dictionary.
ontology.boundary: What this concept is often confused with. Name the confusable, not just 'not X'.
ontology.trigger_signal: Observable signal in user input that this concept is relevant.

frameworks.name: Diagnose Before Fix
frameworks.when_to_use: Use when asked to evaluate or improve in this domain.
steps: Step 3: name the root before suggesting any fix.

core_structure.via: the specific check or framing this domain provides

stances.stance: A prescriptive position that biases agent behavior in this domain.
